cells.name: quotient/periodic
cells.condition: pullbacks along a nontrivial map pi:D->D'; support membership constant on pi-fibers
cells.regime: cyclic quotient towers; complete uniform fibers (A1)

cells.name: dihedral/Chebyshev
cells.condition: quotient cells with an additional involution (inversion u->u^{-1}); Dickson/Chebyshev-detected

cells.name: planted-block
cells.condition: predetermined support positions forced by an algebraically controlled common divisor P
cells.regime: needs a proved subexponential census of the common divisor P (L2405-2407)
cells.profile_count: lem:profile-atlas EXCLUDES arbitrary planted (L4781-4782); could be exponential

cells.name: tangent/deep/common-line
cells.condition: rank-defective contact between the received line and the support-restricted evaluation incidence
cells.regime: fixed low-excess charts + deep regime (thm:deep-regime-upper); general high-excess via prop:tangent-payment

cells.name: extension/field-descent
cells.condition: data defined over a proper subfield (field descent) or factoring only after field extension
cells.regime: deployed Frobenius families; p does not divide |G|
cells.profile_count: slope count = cyclotomic defect p^{d_p(G,I)}, d_p = o(|G|) -> e^{o(n)}

cells.name: differential-locator
cells.condition: locus where the Vandermonde/Hasse Jacobian loses its expected rank
cells.profile_count: differential K-rank defect law proved; inert index=1 under R<=p

cells.name: saturation/effective-image-collapse
cells.condition: passing through displayed images with their actual fiber cardinalities; image << ambient codomain
cells.profile_count: detection exhaustive PROVED; projection degree = binomial tail of e^{Omega(N)} candidate profiles (enumerative input, L2452), OPEN

cells.name: balanced-core/split-pencil
cells.condition: pair of equal-degree monic residual locators with a common depth-w prefix
cells.regime: split pencil (proj dim 1) paid via prop:split-pencil-payment; higher-dim needs proved decomposition or direct ray count (L2473-2474)
cells.profile_count: split pencil: moving-root incidence <= n-g; higher-dim: e^{o(n)} only under (RC) = input 3

cells.name: Fourier/Sidon-heavy analytic
cells.condition: primitive prefix fiber that is too large while having exponentially small additive energy
cells.regime: R>=m (Newton-injective) or fixed m: PROVED-SPECIAL; general linear density m=Theta(N): OPEN
cells.profile_count: prefix-fibre count L <= p^w (#536); Sidon moment payment Gsid=e^{o(Nq)} (def:sidon-paid-cell L5131) UNPROVED at deployed scale

exhaustion.basis: depth-w prefix-fibre total partition (atlas_missing_witness.md #536; w=0 = one-cell binom(D,a) SE2 L3054-3056) + routing detection theorem (routing_exhaustiveness.md #627, c7_routing_spectrum.md #625 MASTER-2 no-third-mode)
exhaustion.note: printed C1-C8 alone need not partition (L2368); C9/primitive is the analytic backstop absorbing up to 27.1% of GENERIC witness mass at toy scale (#536 census). #536 moved (A2)'s coverage clause to payment; #627/#625 upgraded routing detection to a theorem.

summation_over_paid_cells.basis: each of the 5 paid cells has subexponential growth exponent 0; O(1) cell types; max(0,0,0,0,0)=0 -> sum stays e^{o(n)} E_n

summation_over_full_catalogue.basis: lem:profile-atlas excludes arbitrary planted (C3) and higher-dim pencil (C8); C7 projection-degree not payable by enumeration (#626); C9 Sidon payment unproved at deployed scale (input 4/5)

cat_still_needs.C9: hard input 4/5 -- image-normalized Sidon/(MI)/(MA) moment payment; reduced to the R=2 linear-density near-Sidon razor (c9_payment_reduction_map.md)
cat_still_needs.C7_and_C8_higher_dim: hard input 3, condition (RC) -- ray compiler / projection-degree count
cat_still_needs.C3: a subexponential planted census (the only genuinely CAT-local combinatorial item)

unif_dependency.cat_scope: one received line (or one scaled canonical A6 family)
unif_dependency.unif_scope: the SAME paid catalogue holding uniformly in every received line of a genuine asymptotic row sequence (C_n,a_n) as n->infinity
unif_dependency.relation: (UNIF) is the outer universal-over-lines quantifier applied to the (CAT) ledger; (CAT) is its prerequisite. A per-line/per-family payment advances (CAT) for one cell without touching (UNIF); a uniformity argument ignoring the C1-C9 split leaves (CAT) open. No (UNIF) construction attempted here.
